name: glossary
description: Given a 'glossary <term>' request, looks the term up on English Wikipedia and returns a source-grounded definition rendered as a web page. Every answer must cite the exact snapshot line that carries the term; unknown terms return insufficient_evidence instead of a guess.

input.description: A natural-language request of the form 'glossary <term>'.

fields.name: question
fields.description: The request, e.g. 'glossary Mexico'. The term after 'glossary' is looked up.

output.description: Structured JSON with a status, a short definition, source-grounded claims, and the tool calls used. The polished web-page deliverable is rendered from the same grounded snapshot.

tools.name: wikipedia_search
tools.description: Search the offline Wikipedia snapshot cache for a term. Returns {title, file, line, text} matches, best-matching article first.

tools.name: wikipedia_fetch
tools.description: Read an offline Wikipedia snapshot and return its structured article data and citable line.

citationRequirement: Every claim must include at least one citation of the form {file, line}. The cited line must share a keyword with the claim, and when answered the cited line must contain the queried term verbatim.
unsupportedClaimPolicy: If the skill answers, every claim must carry a valid citation into a Wikipedia snapshot. Terms with no cached snapshot must return insufficient_evidence rather than an invented definition.
failureBehavior: When no snapshot matches the term, return status=insufficient_evidence with an empty claims array. Never fabricate a definition, a title, or a citation.
validationRules: Output must match the required JSON schema.
Every citation must reference an existing snapshot file and an in-range line number.
Each cited line must support its claim (shared keyword).
When status is answered, the queried term must appear on a cited line.
Forbidden/hallucinated claims from the test case must not appear in the output.
Required tools must be called, and wikipedia_search must precede wikipedia_fetch.
